title: cornus kousa var. leucotricha
intro: Cornus kousa var. leucotricha, also known as the White Flowering Dogwood, is a variety of dogwood tree that is native to East Asia. Traditionally, the bark and root of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. Cornus kousa var. leucotricha can be prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal uses, although the optimal preparation method and dosage remain unclear.
